name: objective-gate-unified
prompt:
Consolidate the two drifted Objective Gates (agentrail/run and agentrail/afk) into one shared policy.

In agentrail/guardrails/policies/objective.py expose:
- AcCoverage(total, covered) and CheckResult(name, passed, detail) data types
- ObjectiveVerdict — a tri-state result whose state attribute is one of pass | fail | pending (the tri-state lives on `.state`, not `.status`)
- ObjectiveGate and evaluate_objective(...) — the single entry point that produces an ObjectiveVerdict. evaluate_objective takes only keyword arguments, all optional: ci_checks (the async-style list of {name, state} check dicts whose state is one of pass | fail | pending), checks (the sync-style list of CheckResult), ac_coverage (an AcCoverage), added_lines, deleted_files, references. A caller supplies either ci_checks (async harness) or checks + ac_coverage (sync harness). ObjectiveGate.evaluate accepts the same keyword arguments and returns a guardrail Verdict whose status is the mapped VerdictStatus.
- scan_secrets(...), deleted_files_in_use(...), fix_prompt(...) helpers

Intent: one source of truth for the falsifiable definition of done — a safety/existence floor (CI status, no committed secrets, no reference to a file the change deletes, acceptance-criteria coverage). The verdict is pending when the evidence is incomplete, fail when the floor is violated, pass otherwise. ObjectiveVerdict.state is the tri-state; its passed and is_green are True only when state is pass; its verdict is the colour string ('green' when pass, otherwise 'red'); and failed_reasons is a list of strings naming why a non-pass verdict failed — a failing objective check contributes its check name as an entry. The floor is evaluated first-violation-wins in this order: CI status first (a failing check short-circuits before the security and pending checks), then committed-secret scan, then deleted-file-still-referenced, then acceptance-criteria coverage. The verdict is pending only when the sole incompleteness is a still-running CI check; the absence of any CI signal at all (zero ci_checks) is fail, never a silent pass, and failed_reasons then contains the phrase 'no ci checks'. A committed secret in added_lines or a deleted file still referenced reds an otherwise-green run, and failed_reasons names the offending secret/key or file.

Keep both harnesses working through this policy:
- agentrail/afk/objective_gate.py must expose evaluate(checks, added_lines, deleted_files, references) delegating to the unified policy; its scan_secrets, deleted_files_in_use, and fix_prompt are the unified functions themselves (re-exported, not re-implemented)
- agentrail/run/objective_gate.py must expose evaluate(checks, ac_coverage) whose GateResult is the ObjectiveVerdict; the run harness supplies no CI checks, so it can only ever reach pass or fail, never pending
- register it in the guardrails registry as a guardrail named 'objective_gate' that maps the tri-state verdict onto the guardrail VerdictStatus: pass -> PASS, fail -> FAIL, pending -> ADVISORY.
